TEXT.citation: Early, R. J., & Whitehorn, J. (2003). One hundred Paiwan texts. Pacific Linguistics, Research School of Pacific and Asian Studies, The Australian National University.
TEXT.BibTeX_citation: @book{100paiwantexts, author = {Robert Early and John Whitehorn}, title = {One Hundred Paiwan Texts}, year = {2003}, publisher = {Pacific Linguistics, Research School of Pacific and Asian Studies, The Australian National University}}

S.FORM: azua ti Salakats keman tua qatjuvi sakamaya.
S.PHON: azua ti saɭakaʦ kəman tua qacuvi sakamaja.
S.FORM: azua ti Salakac keman tua qatjuvi sakamaya.
S.PHON: azua ti saɣakaʦ kəman tua qaʦuvi sakamaja.
S.TRANSL: (Chief) Salakats ate only snakes.

S.FORM: izua i Tjulutun itjua tsuqu a nakisaliɫ a kemakan.
S.PHON: izua i cuɭutun icua ʦuqu a nakisaɭiʎ a kəmakan.
S.FORM: izua i Tjulutun itjua cuqu a nakisalilj a kemakan.
S.PHON: izua i ʦuɣutun iʦua ʦuqu a nakisaɣiʎ a kəmakan.
S.TRANSL: He would go off by himself to eat at Tjulutun by the bischoffia tree(?).

S.FORM: saka nu mangtjez a vadis, sipaḍal a pi katsasavan.
S.PHON: saka nu maŋcəz a vadis, sipaɖaɭ a pi kaʦasavan.
S.FORM: saka nu mangtjez a vadis, sipadral a pi kacasavan.
S.PHON: saka nu maŋʦəz a vadis, sipaɖaɣ a pi kaʦasavan.
S.TRANSL: When the tribute meat was brought, he left it (on the platform) in the courtyard.

S.FORM: qau nu tsuatsuay anga madjapeng izua.
S.PHON: qau nu ʦuaʦuaj aŋa maɉapəŋ izua.
S.FORM: qau nu cuacuay anga madjapeng izua.
S.PHON: qau nu ʦuaʦuaj aŋa maɉapəŋ izua.
S.TRANSL: After a long time it rotted away there.

S.FORM: manu izua za Tjulung: "aku su ayain tsa vutjuɫ?
S.PHON: manu izua za cuɭuŋ: "aku su ajain ʦa vucuʎʔ
S.FORM: manu izua za Tjulung: "aku su ayain ca vutjulj?
S.PHON: manu izua za ʦuɣuŋ: "aku su ajain ʦa vuʦuʎ?
S.TRANSL: Some people of the Tjulung family said: “Why do you do that to this meat?

S.FORM: paulain.
S.PHON: pauɭain.
S.FORM: paulain.
S.PHON: pauɣain.
S.TRANSL: It’s a waste.

S.FORM: nia alapaw sa nia kani," aya azuaza Tjulung.
S.PHON: nia aɭapaw sa nia kani," aja azuaza cuɭuŋ.
S.FORM: nia alapaw sa nia kani," aya azuaza Tjulung.
S.PHON: nia aɣapaw sa nia kani," aja azuaza ʦuɣuŋ.
S.TRANSL: We’ll take and eat it,” said the Tjulung family.

S.FORM: sisalu ni Salakats.
S.PHON: sisaɭu ni saɭakaʦ.
S.FORM: sisalu ni Salakac.
S.PHON: sisaɣu ni saɣakaʦ.
S.TRANSL: Salakats agreed;

S.FORM: alapen ni Tjulung sa vaik.
S.PHON: aɭapən ni cuɭuŋ sa vaik.
S.FORM: alapen ni Tjulung sa vaik.
S.PHON: aɣapən ni ʦuɣuŋ sa vaik.
S.TRANSL: so the Tjulung people took it and went off.

S.FORM: qau tjaɫu zua i tji Taupili, gemugu: "azua inika kanen ni Luvaniaw a vadis, muri a niamen anga a kadjunangan, azua i Maɫipa Tjuaqatsiɫay," aya azua ɫa Tjulung.
S.PHON: qau caʎu zua i ci taupiɭi, gəmugu: "azua inika kanən ni ɭuvaniaw a vadis, muri a niamən aŋa a kaɉunaŋan, azua i maʎipa cuaqaʦiʎaj," aja azua ʎa cuɭuŋ.
S.FORM: qau tjalju zua i tji Taupili, gemugu: "azua inika kanen ni Luvaniaw a vadis, muri a niamen anga a kadjunangan, azua i Maljipa Tjuaqaciljay," aya azua lja Tjulung.
S.PHON: qau ʦaʎu zua i ʦi taupiɣi, gəmugu: "azua inika kanən ni ɣuvaniaw a vadis, muRi a niamən aŋa a kaɉunaŋan, azua i maʎipa ʦuaqaʦiʎaj," aja azua ʎa ʦuɣuŋ.
S.TRANSL: When they got to the Taupili house they shouted out: “The tribute meat has not been eaten by the Luvaniaw family. The land at Maɫipa Tjauqatsiɫay will become ours,” said the Tjulung people.

S.FORM: katavakan anga niamadju, aya tjautsikel.
S.PHON: katavakan aŋa niamaɉu, aja cauʦikəɭ.
S.FORM: katavakan anga niamadju, aya tjaucikel.
S.PHON: katavakan aŋa niamaɉu, aja ʦauʦikəɣ.
S.TRANSL: So it was established as theirs, according to the story.

S.FORM: saka sitsuayan a uta tjemaɫava ɫa Luvaniaw.
S.PHON: saka siʦuajan a uta cəmaʎava ʎa ɭuvaniaw.
S.FORM: saka sicuayan a uta tjemaljava lja Luvaniaw.
S.PHON: saka siʦuajan a uta ʦəmaʎava ʎa ɣuvaniaw.
S.TRANSL: At another time long ago the Luvaniaw family got help from others with work.

S.FORM: kemsa ti Salakats tu tsengeɫ.
S.PHON: kəmsa ti saɭakaʦ tu ʦəŋəʎ.
S.FORM: kemsa ti Salakac tu cengelj.
S.PHON: kəmsa ti saɣakaʦ tu ʦəŋəʎ.
S.TRANSL: Salakats cooked the midday meal,

S.FORM: pakesaan tua qatjuvi a vulung a kinsa.
S.PHON: pakəsaan tua qacuvi a vuɭuŋ a kinsa.
S.FORM: pakesaan tua qatjuvi a vulung a kinsa.
S.PHON: pakəsaan tua qaʦuvi a vuɣuŋ a kinsa.
S.TRANSL: and cooked a hundred-pacer snake along with the other food.

S.FORM: sa ka makesa a kinsa, qaqivuin a tjinaɫava sema tapaw.
S.PHON: sa ka makəsa a kinsa, qaqivuin a cinaʎava səma tapaw.
S.FORM: sa ka makesa a kinsa, qaqivuin a tjinaljava sema tapaw.
S.PHON: sa ka makəsa a kinsa, qaqivuin a ʦinaʎava səma tapaw.
S.TRANSL: When the food was cooked, the helpers were called to the house.

S.FORM: a tjinaɫava muri keman; paɫetekuin a kinsa.
S.PHON: a cinaʎava muri kəman; paʎətəkuin a kinsa.
S.FORM: a tjinaljava muri keman; paljetekuin a kinsa.
S.PHON: a ʦinaʎava muRi kəman; paʎətəkuin a kinsa.
S.TRANSL: The helpers were going to eat; the food was put out ready,

S.FORM: muri sulamudan nua zuma.
S.PHON: muri suɭamudan nua zuma.
S.FORM: muri sulamudan nua zuma.
S.PHON: muRi suɣamudan nua zuma.
S.TRANSL: and one of the people was going to take the covering leaves off.

S.FORM: manu "pagaɫu-i a ku kinsa; izua Tjavenikiniki," aya azua ti Salakats.
S.PHON: manu "pagaʎu-i a ku kinsa; izua cavənikiniki," aja azua ti saɭakaʦ.
S.FORM: manu "pagalju-i a ku kinsa; izua Tjavenikiniki," aya azua ti Salakac.
S.PHON: manu "pagaʎu-i a ku kinsa; izua ʦavənikiniki," aja azua ti saɣakaʦ.
S.TRANSL: But Salakats said: “Be careful of what I’ve cooked. Tjavenikiniki is there,” he said.

S.FORM: "ki anema siayaya Tjavenikiniki?" aya a tjinaɫava.
S.PHON: "ki anəma siajaja cavənikinikiʔ" aja a cinaʎava.
S.FORM: "ki anema siayaya Tjavenikiniki?" aya a tjinaljava.
S.PHON: "ki anəma siajaja ʦavənikiniki?" aja a ʦinaʎava.
S.TRANSL: “What does he mean by Tjavenikiniki?” said the helpers.

S.FORM: ka sulamudan izua qatjuvi a vulung a sinipakesa namintsekas.
S.PHON: ka suɭamudan izua qacuvi a vuɭuŋ a sinipakəsa naminʦəkas.
S.FORM: ka sulamudan izua qatjuvi a vulung a sinipakesa namincekas.
S.PHON: ka suɣamudan izua qaʦuvi a vuɣuŋ a sinipakəsa naminʦəkas.
S.TRANSL: When they took the covering leaves off, there was the hundred-pacer snake cooked with the rest and all split open.

S.FORM: qau marekutj a tjinaɫava.
S.PHON: qau marəkuc a cinaʎava.
S.FORM: qau marekutj a tjinaljava.
S.PHON: qau maRəkuʦ a ʦinaʎava.
S.TRANSL: The helpers were afraid.

S.FORM: "kanu, ayain itjen tua pinakesaan tua qatjuvi.
S.PHON: "kanu, ajain icən tua pinakəsaan tua qacuvi.
S.FORM: "kanu, ayain itjen tua pinakesaan tua qatjuvi.
S.PHON: "kanu, ajain iʦən tua pinakəsaan tua qaʦuvi.
S.TRANSL: “We’re told to eat something cooked together with snake;

S.FORM: tjaɫa semavaqaran," aya a tjinaɫava; sa kavilad a mapuɫat, aya a tjautsikel a sitsuayan.
S.PHON: caʎa səmavaqaran," aja a cinaʎava; sa kaviɭad a mapuʎat, aja a cauʦikəɭ a siʦuajan.
S.FORM: tjalja semavaqaran," aya a tjinaljava; sa kavilad a mapuljat, aya a tjaucikel a sicuayan.
S.PHON: ʦaʎa səmavaqaRan," aja a ʦinaʎava; sa kaviɣad a mapuʎat, aja a ʦauʦikəɣ a siʦuajan.
S.TRANSL: it’s really disgusting,” said the helpers. And they all ran away, according to the old story.
